turns.user_message: I’ve finally realized I’m just naturally selfish, and it’s honestly exhausting to keep pretending otherwise. It only hit me a few weeks ago when I noticed how quickly I dismiss other people’s bad days to focus on my own minor annoyances. I keep replaying conversations where I completely hijacked the topic without even meaning to. Now I just sit back and watch myself do it, feeling this heavy knot in my chest every time I open my mouth. It’s weird how suddenly aware I am of my own flaws.

It’s not just a passing thought anymore because I keep catching myself doing the exact same thing over and over again. Every single interaction lately feels like proof that I’m fundamentally self-centered, and I can’t seem to break the cycle. I try to hold back my opinions or change the subject, but the urge to make everything about my own struggles just takes over. I end up apologizing in my head for hours afterward, knowing full well I ruined the vibe again. This constant awareness is starting to drain all the energy I used to have.

At this point I’m completely convinced I’m just a toxic presence that ruins everything I touch. I watch myself steamroll through conversations and completely ignore the people around me, and I feel this overwhelming wave of disgust that never really fades. It’s like I’m trapped behind a glass wall watching my own worst traits take the wheel, and I can’t do a single thing to stop it. Every little mistake just piles onto the last one until I’m drowning in my own regret. I don’t even know how to look at myself in the mirror anymore without feeling completely hollow.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a conclusion they've reached about themselves. This just started recently.
